Out-of-order RV32IM · Chisel · ChiselSim / Verilator

DOOM, running on a CPU I built

Shareware doom1.wad. No emulator underneath — the game is executing on an original 1-wide out-of-order RISC-V core, rendered pixel by pixel through its framebuffer. Scripted keyboard input walks it from the title screen into live gameplay on E1M1.

0.79IPC in render loop
2.5%Branch mispredict
0.2%Divider busy
58Frames captured
RV32IM48/48 ISA tests
  1. Title screen
  2. Enter → menu
  3. New Game
  4. Episode 1
  5. Skill → level
  6. Walking E1M1

Every pixel above was produced by the core's own store instructions to the framebuffer MMIO region, mirrored out of the simulator and written straight to PNG. The player runs entirely offline; all 58 frames are embedded in this file.
